species.citation: (Blume) M.Roem. , Fam. Nat. Syn. Monogr. 2 (1846) 117 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 19 (2010) 26; ≡ Zanonia macrocarpa Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 937; ≡ Macrozanonia macrocarpa (Blume) Cogn. , Bull. Herb. Boissier, 1st Sér. 1 (1893) 612 ; Cogn. in Engl., Pflanzenreich 66 (1916) 262; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 578; Type: from Java. = Macrozanonia philippinensis (Merr.) Cogn. , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (275 I) (1916) 264; ≡ Zanonia philippinensis Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 141; Type: Clemens 324 (PNH, holo), Philippines. = Erythropalum triandrum Quisumb. & Merr. , PJS 37 (1928) 143; Type: Ramos & Edaño BS 49096 (PNH, holo; K, iso), Mindanao: Davao: Mati, 26-Mar-1927.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BILIRAN, LUZON: Laguna, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Norte (photos), Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, NEGROS.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation primary forests. 0-800m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 23 October 2017

species.citation: (Parkinson) W.J.de Wilde & Duyfjes , Reinwardtia 12 (2008) 268 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 19 (2010) 40; ≡ Cucurbita pruriens Parkinson , J. Voy. South Seas (1773) 44. forma hispida (Thunb.) W.J.de Wilde & Duyfjes, Sandakania 17 (2008) 47; ≡ Benincasa hispida (Thunb.) Cogn., Monogr. Phan. 3 (1881) 513; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 583; ≡ Cucurbita hispida Thunb., Nova Acta Regiae Soc. Sci. Upsal., 2nd Ser. 4 (1783) 38. Distribution: Australia, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. Notes: Cultivated for its large edible fruit in the Philippines, occasionally escaping. Wax gourd or winter melon; Kondol. Naturalized.
species.distribution: Australia, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. LUZON: Rizal (photos), PALAWAN.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (23-Oct-2017): Some seem to consider Cucurbita pruriens Parkinson an invalid name, and therefore use Benincasa hispida (Thunb.) Cogn. for this species, but it is unclear to me why this would be the case. 0-700m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 18 November 2020

species.citation: (Thunb.) Matsum. & Nakai , Index Seminum (Tokyo) (1916) 30 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 19 (2010) 49; ≡ Momordica lanata Thunb. , Prodr. Pl. Cap. species.distribution: Africa.
species.notes: Cultivated pantropically. Grown in many parts of the Philippines, not uncommonly escaping. Waste places. Watermelon.

species.distribution: Philippines. BORACAY (photos), CEBU, LUZON: La Union (photos), NCR (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Sur (photos), PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Not reported for the Philippines by Holstein (2015), who noted that it is unclear if Coccinia grandis is native to Malesia. 0-400m.

species.notes: Cultivated in all warm countries. Cultivated in several forms or varieties in the Philippines, occasionally escaping. Common melon.

species.distribution: Bangladesh, China, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Thailand.
species.notes: Planted in all warm countries. Generally cultivated in the Philippines. Cucumber. Not naturalized?

species.notes: Planted in all warm countries. Widely cultivated in the Philippines, occasionally escaping. Squash. Kalabasa.

species.notes: Squash, pumpkin. Cultivated. Not naturalized?

W.J.de Wilde & Duyfjes, Reinwardtia 12 (2009) 405; ≡ Ilocania pedata Merr., PJS 13c (1918) 65; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 581; Type: Ramos BS 27552 (US, lecto), Philippines, Ilocos Norte, Bangui. species.notes: Cultivated in the Old World tropics. In the Philippines commonly grown for its edible fruits. Patola.

species.distribution: Bangladesh, India. LUZON: Rizal (photos).
species.notes: Pantropic in cultivation. Commonly grown for its edible fruits, the fruits being larger than the very common wild form which occurs in thickets in the settled areas throughout the Philippines.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 35 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Blumea 51 (2006) 10; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 19 (2010) 107; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987.

species.notes: At low elevation along roadsides, cultivated lands, etc. Fl. & fr. throughout the year.

Haines, which is considered by some as synonymous with Trichosanthes cucumerina subsp. cucumerina. 0-1200m. Native.
